title: Cranachan
subtitle: Toasted oats, cream, whisky, honey, and raspberries
excerpt: A Scottish dessert of whipped cream folded with toasted oatmeal, honey, and whisky, layered with fresh raspberries. It is assembled at the table in some traditions — the components brought separately for each person to mix. The toasted oatmeal gives texture that most creamy desserts lack.
sourceNotes: Cranachan is a traditional Scottish harvest festival dessert, originally made with crowdie (a fresh Scottish cheese) rather than cream. The cream version has been standard since at least the 19th century. It is served at Burns Night, Hogmanay, and summer gatherings when raspberries are in season. Raspberries from Tayside are considered the best in Britain.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Toast the oats a day ahead. Assemble just before serving — the cream softens the oats quickly.

content.text: Toast the oatmeal in a dry frying pan until it is golden and fragrant — watch it carefully, it colours quickly. Cool completely before folding into the cream. Hot oats will deflate the whipped cream.

The cream should be whipped to soft peaks, not stiff. Overly stiff cream makes the cranachan heavy. The whisky and honey are folded in after the cream is whipped — they thin the cream slightly, which is correct.

items.prepNote: toasted in a dry pan until golden

content.text: Whip the {{double-cream}} to soft peaks. Fold in the {{honey}} and {{whisky}} gently. Fold in the cooled toasted {{porridge-oats}}. Reserve a few {{raspberries}} for the top. Layer the cream mixture and raspberries alternately in glasses or bowls, finishing with a spoonful of cream, a few raspberries, a drizzle of honey, and a small scattering of toasted oats. Serve immediately.

items.symptom: Oats immediately soft and losing their crunch
items.cause: Added while still warm, or assembled too far in advance
items.fix: Cool the oats completely before folding in. Assemble no more than 30 minutes before serving
Whip to soft, flowing peaks only. The whisky and honey thin the cream further once folded in

content.text: Cranachan is Scotland's national dessert, eaten at Burns Night, Hogmanay, and at any summer gathering when raspberries are at their peak. The assembly-at-table tradition makes it a social dessert — the components are brought out separately and each person mixes their own. The whisky quantity is a personal choice.
